home.brandsSubtitle: We work with the world's most trusted implant systems
home.brand1Desc: Ideal for full arch, immediate loading
home.whySubtitle: The values that set us apart
home.why1Desc: Over 3000 successful implant procedures — one of the most experienced implantologists in Azerbaijan.

home.faq1Q: How long does a dental implant last?
home.faq1A: With proper care, they can last a lifetime. The titanium implant fuses with the bone through osseointegration, creating a permanent root.
home.faq2A: The surgery is performed under local anesthesia — no pain is felt. Sedation is also available.
home.faq3A: The surgery itself takes 45–90 minutes. Bone fusion takes 2–4 months, then the permanent crown is placed.
home.faq4A: Generally, healthy adults with sufficient bone volume can get implants. A CT scan provides precise evaluation.
home.faq5Q: How much does an implant cost?
home.faq5A: Cost varies depending on the brand, bone condition, and treatment plan. Come for a free consultation for an exact quote.
home.ctaSubtitle: Start with a free initial consultation. We'll create a personalized treatment plan.

implantasiya.intro: A dental implant is a medical-grade titanium screw that replaces the root of a missing tooth. Fusing with the bone, it creates a permanent foundation for a ceramic crown.
implantasiya.whatIsDesc: A dental implant is a medical titanium post surgically placed into the jawbone. Over time, bone grows around the implant — this process is called osseointegration. After full integration, a ceramic crown is attached. The result is a lifetime restoration indistinguishable from a natural tooth in appearance, function, and feel.
implantasiya.step1Desc: Bone structure, volume, and implant placement position are precisely measured with 3D imaging.
implantasiya.step2Desc: The implant angle, depth, and distance from critical structures are simulated before surgery.
implantasiya.step3Desc: 45–90 minute procedure under local anesthesia. Sedation option available.
implantasiya.step4Desc: 2–4 months for bone fusion. A temporary prosthesis is used during this period.
implantasiya.step5Desc: After confirming fusion, an individual ceramic crown or bridge is attached.
implantasiya.benefit1: Lifetime service — 25+ years with proper care
implantasiya.faq1Q: How many years does an implant last?
implantasiya.faq1A: With proper care, it lasts a lifetime. Research shows 95%+ of implants function successfully for 15+ years.
implantasiya.faq2A: No pain under local anesthesia. Mild discomfort may occur for 2–3 days after surgery.
implantasiya.faq3Q: What can I eat after implant placement?
implantasiya.faq3A: Soft foods are recommended for the first 3–5 days. Hard foods are limited during osseointegration.
implantasiya.faq4Q: Can I get an implant with insufficient bone?
implantasiya.faq4A: Yes. Bone grafting (GBR) or sinus lift creates the necessary volume.
implantasiya.ctaDesc: Call today for a 3D CT scan and individual treatment plan.

allOn4.intro: All-on-4 technology provides a complete jaw prosthesis on just 4 implants — the least invasive and fastest solution for complete tooth loss.
allOn4.whatIsDesc: All-on-4 uses just 4 implants instead of the traditional 8–12 required to restore a full jaw. The rear 2 implants are placed at an angle, covering a larger area of bone. A temporary fixed prosthesis is placed on the day of surgery.
allOn4.candidate1: Complete tooth loss in one or both jaws
allOn4.candidate4: Patients with low bone density (in most cases)
allOn4.benefit1: Temporary fixed teeth on the day of surgery
allOn4.benefit3: In most cases, no bone grafting needed
allOn4.benefit5: Immediate improvement in quality of life
allOn4.step4: Temporary prosthesis on the same day
allOn4.faq1Q: What is the difference between All-on-4 and All-on-6?
allOn4.faq1A: All-on-6 uses 6 implants for wider distribution and greater load capacity. Choice depends on bone condition.
allOn4.faq2Q: Will there be teeth on the day of surgery?
allOn4.faq2A: Yes. In most cases, a temporary fixed prosthesis is placed on the day of surgery.
allOn4.faq3Q: How long does the surgery take?
allOn4.faq3A: Approximately 2–3 hours per jaw. Both jaws can be treated on the same day.
allOn4.ctaDesc: Call today for a free evaluation.

sumukArtirmasi.heroSubtitle: Insufficient Bone Is No Longer an Obstacle
sumukArtirmasi.intro: When there is insufficient bone volume for an implant, bone grafting (GBR) creates the necessary conditions.
sumukArtirmasi.whatIsDesc: Bone grafting (GBR — Guided Bone Regeneration) is a surgical procedure used when the jawbone lacks sufficient volume for implant placement. Autogenous, allogeneic, or xenograft materials are used to restore bone volume. After 3–6 months, once bone formation is confirmed, the implant is placed.
sumukArtirmasi.step1Desc: Bone volume is precisely measured with 3D imaging, deficiency is identified.
sumukArtirmasi.step2Desc: Autogenous, allogeneic, or xenograft material is selected based on clinical situation.
sumukArtirmasi.step3Desc: Material is applied, covered with membrane, sutures placed. Under local anesthesia.
sumukArtirmasi.step4Desc: 3–6 months for new bone to form. A temporary prosthesis may be used during this time.
sumukArtirmasi.step5Desc: Once bone formation is confirmed, the implant is placed.
sumukArtirmasi.faq1A: Performed under local anesthesia, pain is minimal. Swelling for 3–5 days after surgery.
sumukArtirmasi.faq2Q: How long does the procedure take?
sumukArtirmasi.faq2A: 3–6 months for bone formation, then implantation is carried out.
sumukArtirmasi.faq3Q: Where does the bone material come from?
sumukArtirmasi.faq3A: From your own bone (autogenous), from a donor (allogeneic), or from animal material (xenograft).
sumukArtirmasi.ctaDesc: A CT scan will determine your bone volume and we'll create a treatment plan.

sinusLift.heroSubtitle: The Problem of Upper Jaw Posterior Implants Solved
sinusLift.intro: When there is insufficient bone in the posterior upper jaw, a sinus lift creates the necessary bone volume.
sinusLift.whatIsDesc: A sinus lift is an operation in which the sinus membrane is elevated and bone material is placed underneath. This creates the necessary bone depth for implant placement in the posterior upper jaw.
sinusLift.type1Desc: Side entry for significant bone augmentation (5–15 mm increase). Applied when very little bone is present. Performed 4–6 months before implantation.
sinusLift.type2Desc: Minimal entry through the implant site (2–4 mm increase). Can be performed simultaneously with implant placement when sufficient bone exists.
sinusLift.faq1Q: How long does a sinus lift operation take?
sinusLift.faq1A: The lateral method takes 60–90 minutes; the osteotome technique is shorter, done together with implantation.
sinusLift.faq2Q: How long should I rest after a sinus lift?
sinusLift.faq2A: 3–5 days of rest is recommended. Avoid strenuous exercise for 2 weeks.
sinusLift.faq3A: Risk is minimal when performed by an experienced surgeon. The most common complication is sinus membrane perforation, which is detected and resolved during surgery.
sinusLift.ctaDesc: We'll create a personalized plan for your upper jaw.

sedasiya.intro: For patients who fear dentists — painless implantation under conscious sedation. You remain aware, but without pain or anxiety.
sedasiya.whatIsDesc: Conscious sedation is a method in which the patient remains awake, breathing independently, but in a state of deep relaxation. Combined with local anesthesia, no pain or anxiety is felt during surgery. The patient recovers quickly after the procedure.
sedasiya.candidate3: Those with a strong gag reflex
sedasiya.candidate4: When multiple implants are placed at once
sedasiya.benefit1: Pain and anxiety reduced to zero
sedasiya.faq1Q: How does sedation differ from general anesthesia?
sedasiya.faq1A: With general anesthesia, the patient is completely unconscious. With sedation, they're semi-drowsy but breathing independently. Safer with faster recovery.
sedasiya.faq2Q: How long should I rest after sedation?
sedasiya.faq2A: A few hours of rest is recommended. Driving and important decisions are not advised on the same day.
sedasiya.faq3Q: Is special preparation needed for sedation?
sedasiya.faq3A: It is recommended not to eat for 6 hours before the procedure. The doctor will provide detailed instructions.
sedasiya.ctaDesc: Learn more about implantation under sedation.

haqqimda.intro: Pioneer of digital implantology in Azerbaijan. 3000+ successful implants, 15+ years of clinical experience.
haqqimda.bioText: Dr. Bakhtiyar Aliyev graduated with distinction from Azerbaijan Medical University's Faculty of Dentistry. He completed clinical internships at leading dental institutions in Germany, Italy, and Turkey, specializing in contemporary implantology, digital prosthodontics, and smile design. He is currently the founder and lead dentist of 'Smile by Dr. Bakhtiyar' clinic in Baku, operating according to European standards.
haqqimda.edu1: Azerbaijan Medical University, Faculty of Dentistry — With Distinction
haqqimda.edu2: Clinical internship in Germany — modern implantology
haqqimda.edu3: Clinical internship in Italy — aesthetic dentistry
haqqimda.edu4: Clinical internship in Turkey — digital prosthodontics
haqqimda.cert6: Member of European Academy of Aesthetic Dentistry
haqqimda.philosophyText: An individual approach to each patient's unique needs is the foundation of my clinical philosophy. Combining modern digital technologies, the world's best implant systems, and 15+ years of accumulated clinical experience, I offer every patient a lifetime smile solution.
haqqimda.ctaDesc: Call today for an initial consultation.

qiymetler.intro: The exact cost of a dental implant cannot be determined without clinical evaluation, CT scan, and treatment planning. Below are the factors that affect pricing.
qiymetler.factor1Desc: Price ranges vary from NeoBiotech to premium Straumann.
qiymetler.factor2Desc: If bone grafting or sinus lift is needed, additional costs are added.
qiymetler.factor3Desc: Single crown, bridge, or full jaw (All-on-4) fall into different price categories.
qiymetler.factor4Desc: Ranging from a single implant to complex full restoration cases.
qiymetler.factor5Desc: 3D CT scan and digital planning are essential for precise results.
qiymetler.consultDesc: Visit our clinic for an exact cost. Based on CT scan results, an individual treatment plan and cost are calculated. The initial consultation is free.
qiymetler.whyNoFixedDesc: Each patient's bone condition, needs, and chosen implant system are different. A fixed price list is often misleading. We provide each patient with an individual, transparent quote.
qiymetler.ctaDesc: Call today or message on WhatsApp. Free initial consultation.

sualCavab.heroSubtitle: Answers to the Most Common Questions

items.q: How long does a dental implant last?
items.a: With proper care, a lifetime. Research shows 95%+ of implants function successfully for 15+ years.
Surgery under local anesthesia — no pain. Sedation available. Mild discomfort possible for 2–3 days after.
Surgery: 45–90 minutes. Full treatment process (including osseointegration): 3–6 months.
Healthy adults with sufficient bone volume. Precise evaluation via CT scan. Some medical conditions may limit implantation.

items.q: How much does an implant cost?
items.a: Cost depends on the system, bone condition, and treatment plan. Exact cost at a free consultation.

items.q: Can I get an implant with insufficient bone?
items.a: Yes. Bone grafting (GBR) creates the necessary volume. Sinus lift is used for the upper jaw.
All-on-4 is a fixed full jaw prosthesis on 4 implants. The most modern, least invasive solution for complete tooth loss. Temporary teeth on the same day.

items.q: What can I eat after implant placement?
items.a: Soft foods (yogurt, soup, mashed potatoes) for the first 3–5 days. Hard foods avoided during osseointegration.

items.q: How do I care for my implant?
items.a: Brush twice daily, use interdental brushes and antiseptic mouthwash. Annual dental check-ups are essential.
Titanium implants are MRI-compatible. They don't cause problems with medical imaging.

items.q: When can an implant be placed after tooth extraction?
items.a: 3–6 months after extraction, once bone has healed. Immediate implantation is possible in some cases.
Conscious sedation keeps the patient semi-drowsy but breathing independently. Ideal for anxious patients.
In rare cases, osseointegration can fail. Smoking, poor oral hygiene, and certain conditions increase risk. Our success rate is 98%.

items.q: How many implants do I need?
items.a: Varies — from 1 implant for a single missing tooth to All-on-4 (4 implants) for a full jaw.

items.q: How do I book an appointment?
items.a: By phone (+994 10 501 01 07), via WhatsApp, or by filling out the booking form on our website.

elaqe.heroSubtitle: We're ready to answer your questions
